q.en: How did Sarah feel on the morning of the first day of school?
q.zh: 开学第一天早上，莎拉(Sarah)的感觉是怎样的？

options.en: Apprehensive and nervous
options.zh: 忐忑不安、很紧张

options.en: Excited and happy according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 兴奋又开心（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Angry and upset
options.zh: 生气又烦躁

options.en: Bored and sleepy
options.zh: 无聊又困倦

explain.en: The very first sentence says, 'Sarah had never been so apprehensive about school before,' and she feels a flutter in her tummy and sweaty palms.
explain.zh: 故事第一句就写：「莎拉以前从没对学校这么忐忑不安过」，而且她肚子里翻腾、手心冒汗。

q.en: At the end of the story, who is Sarah?
q.zh: 在故事结尾，莎拉到底是做什么的？

options.en: The school principal
options.zh: 学校校长

options.en: The new teacher, Ms. Parker
options.zh: 新来的老师，帕克(Ms. Parker)女士

options.en: A parent dropping off a child according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 送孩子上学的家长（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: A new student
options.zh: 一名新学生

explain.en: The twist is at the end: after the bell, Sarah stands up and says, 'My name is Ms. Parker, and I'm going to be your teacher this year.' She is the teacher, not a student.
explain.zh: 故事的转折在结尾：铃响后莎拉站起来说：「我叫帕克女士，今年将由我担任你们的老师。」她是老师，不是学生。

q.en: What did Sarah's brother Kent do that morning?
q.zh: 那天早上，莎拉的弟弟肯特(Kent)做了什么？

options.en: He helped Sarah pick out new clothes according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 他帮莎拉挑选新衣服（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: He stayed home sick
options.zh: 他生病在家没去上学

options.en: He ate Frosted Flakes, dashed out to catch the bus, and left his bowl on the table
options.zh: 他吃了脆麦片、冲出门去赶校车，还把碗留在桌上

options.en: He drove Sarah to school
options.zh: 他开车送莎拉上学

explain.en: The book says, 'Kent had already wolfed down his Frosted Flakes and dashed out to catch the bus... He'd left his dirty bowl on the kitchen table, with three Frosted Flakes crusted on its rim.'
explain.zh: 书里说：「肯特已经狼吞虎咽吃完脆麦片、冲出门去赶校车了……他把脏碗留在厨房桌上，碗沿还粘着三片脆麦片。」

q.en: Why was Sarah new to this school and town?
q.zh: 为什么莎拉对这所学校和这个镇子来说是「新来的」？

options.en: She had graduated and returned
options.zh: 她毕业后又回来了

options.en: She had just moved to a new house in a new town
options.zh: 她刚搬到一个新镇子、住进新房子里

options.en: The school had just been built
options.zh: 这所学校刚建成

options.en: She had been away on a long trip according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 她出了一趟远门刚回来（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says, 'Sarah had just moved to this new house in a new town,' and she keeps forgetting where the food and dishes are kept.
explain.zh: 书里说：「莎拉刚搬进这个新镇子的新房子里」，而且她总记不清食物和餐具放在哪里。

q.en: The story is told mostly through Sarah's thoughts and feelings. From what point of view is it written?
q.zh: 这个故事主要通过莎拉的想法和感受来讲述。它是用哪种视角（人称）写的？

options.en: Third-person, following Sarah's thoughts and feelings
options.zh: 第三人称——跟随莎拉的想法和感受

options.en: Second-person, speaking to 'you'
options.zh: 第二人称——以「你」来称呼读者

options.en: First-person, with Sarah saying 'I' according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 第一人称——由莎拉以「我」的口吻讲述（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: As a script performed on stage
options.zh: 一出剧本的形式

explain.en: The narrator uses 'Sarah had never been so apprehensive...' and describes her inner thoughts from outside, which is third-person point of view.
explain.zh: 叙述者用「莎拉以前从没这么忐忑不安……」从外部描写她的内心想法，这是第三人称视角。

q.en: What does the phrase 'the butterflies in her stomach' mean in the story?
q.zh: 在故事里，「肚子里有蝴蝶」(butterflies in her stomach) 这句话是什么意思？

options.en: She was hungry for breakfast
options.zh: 她饿着想吃早饭

options.en: There were real insects in the house according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 屋子里有真的昆虫（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: She had actually eaten butterflies
options.zh: 她真的吃了蝴蝶

options.en: Sarah felt nervous and fluttery inside
options.zh: 莎拉心里紧张、七上八下

explain.en: 'The butterflies in her stomach were too active this morning' is a figurative way of saying Sarah felt very nervous, not that real butterflies were there.
explain.zh: 「今天早上她肚子里的蝴蝶太活跃了」是一种比喻，意思是莎拉非常紧张，并不是真的有蝴蝶。

q.en: Inside herself, Sarah balanced 'two opposing feelings.' What were they?
q.zh: 莎拉心里同时平衡着「两种相反的感觉」。它们是什么？

options.en: Anger and peace
options.zh: 愤怒与平静

options.en: Hunger and fullness
options.zh: 饥饿与饱胀

options.en: Nervousness that made her energetic and fidgety, and reluctance that made her slow and clumsy
options.zh: 一种是让她亢奋、坐立不安的紧张；另一种是让她迟缓、笨手笨脚的迟疑

options.en: Sleepiness and energy according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book as mentioned in a later chapter
options.zh: 困倦与精力充沛（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）（正如后文某一章提到的）

explain.en: The book says she was 'balancing two opposing feelings... one was a nervousness that made her energetic and fidgety, and the other was a reluctance that made her slow and clumsy.'
explain.zh: 书里说她「平衡着两种相反的感觉……一种是让她亢奋、坐立不安的紧张，另一种是让她迟缓、笨手笨脚的迟疑」。

q.en: The author mentions a ketchup stain on Sarah's shirt and threadbare cuffs. What do these details tell us?
q.zh: 作者提到莎拉衬衫上有番茄酱污渍、袖口也磨破了。这些细节说明了什么？

options.en: Sarah worried about how she looked and whether others would notice her worn clothes
options.zh: 莎拉很在意自己的外表，担心别人会注意到她磨损的衣服

options.en: Sarah was very messy and careless according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 莎拉又邋遢又粗心（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: Sarah loved eating French fries
options.zh: 莎拉特别爱吃炸薯条

options.en: Sarah's clothes were brand new
options.zh: 莎拉的衣服是全新的

explain.en: She hoped no one would notice the ketchup stain, scolded herself for not buying a new outfit, and rubbed her cuffs threadbare — all showing her worry about her appearance on a big day.
explain.zh: 她希望没人注意到番茄酱污渍，还责怪自己没买新衣服，袖口也被她紧张地搓得磨破了——这些都说明她在乎自己在这个重要日子的外表。

q.en: What is one reason the author, Katherine Follett, wrote this story?
q.zh: 作者凯瑟琳·福莱特(Katherine Follett)写这个故事，其中一个原因是什么？

options.en: To explain how school buildings are constructed according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 为了讲解学校大楼是怎么建成的（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: To teach readers how to cook French fries
options.zh: 为了教读者怎么做炸薯条

options.en: To warn children not to talk to teachers
options.zh: 为了警告孩子别和老师说话

options.en: To show that even a teacher can feel nervous on a first day, and to help readers feel empathy
options.zh: 为了展现即使是老师，开学第一天也会紧张，并让读者产生共鸣

explain.en: By building Sarah's nervousness and then revealing she is the teacher, the author helps readers understand that new beginnings are scary for everyone — even the person in charge.
explain.zh: 作者先铺陈莎拉的紧张，再揭示她就是老师，目的是让读者明白：新的开始对每个人——哪怕是站在讲台上的人——都可能是可怕的。

q.en: Why does the author wait until the very end to reveal that Sarah is the teacher, not a student?
q.zh: 作者为什么直到最后才揭晓：莎拉是老师，而不是学生？

options.en: To hide that the story takes place at a school according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 为了隐瞒故事发生在学校里（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: To surprise the reader and build a deeper understanding of how it feels to be the new person
options.zh: 为了给读者一个惊喜，并让人更深刻地体会「新来的人」是什么感受

options.en: To make the story confusing on purpose
options.zh: 为了故意把故事写得让人看不懂

options.en: Because the author forgot to say it earlier
options.zh: 因为作者前面忘了交代

explain.en: The delayed reveal is a deliberate choice: readers assume Sarah is a student, so her nervousness feels universal, and the twist creates both surprise and empathy.
explain.zh: 这种「延迟揭晓」是刻意的：读者会以为莎拉是学生，于是她的紧张显得人人都有；而结尾的转折既带来惊喜，也唤起了共鸣。

prompt.en: For most of the story, we think Sarah is a nervous new student — but she turns out to be the new teacher. Think of a time you were the 'new person' somewhere (a new school, team, class, or neighborhood) and felt fluttery or unsure. What helped you feel more comfortable, and what would you say to someone else who is new and nervous?
prompt.zh: 故事大部分时间里，我们都以为莎拉是紧张的新生——结果她却是新来的老师。回想一次你成为某个地方「新来的人」的经历（新学校、新球队、新班级或新社区），当时是否也感到忐忑不安？是什么让你慢慢放松下来的？你会对另一个新来又紧张的人说些什么？

en: Sarah reminded herself, 'Being new is always difficult... but eventually everyone makes friends' (p. 4).
She tried to relax by telling herself the other students 'just assume you're going somewhere else' (p. 5).
A good answer gives a real personal example of being new and offers kind, specific advice to someone else in that spot.
zh: 莎拉安慰自己：「新来总是很难的……但最后大家都会交到朋友」（第4页）。
她试着放松，告诉自己其他学生「只是以为你要去别的地方」（第5页）。
好的回答要举一个真实的「新来」经历，并给身处同样处境的人具体、友善的建议。
